blockers.description: Wave 2 requires the user to run build.ps1 on a clean Windows 11 VM snapshot with no system GStreamer and paste stdout/stderr back into chat. Claude runs on Linux and cannot execute PyInstaller for Windows cross-platform.
blockers.workaround: none — this is the spike's intended execution model per D-09

human_actions_pending.action: On clean Win11 VM snapshot: install GStreamer 1.24.12 MSVC runtime + devel MSIs (paths in README.md), populate .planning/phases/43-gstreamer-windows-spike/test_url.txt with one AudioAddict HTTPS channel URL from your library (one line — gitignored, will NOT be committed), run `.\build.ps1`, paste the full artifacts\smoke.log into chat plus a one-word audibility verdict ('audible' or 'silent').
human_actions_pending.context: Wave 2 of the spike. D-01 requires a clean snapshot (no system GStreamer on PATH). D-07 requires a real AA URL from the user's library. D-09 specifies Claude writes artifacts / user executes / paste-back drives diagnosis.

decisions.decision: Pin GStreamer 1.24.12 MSVC x86_64 as the Windows bundling target
decisions.rationale: Final 1.24.x stable (Jan 2025); MSVC build has better PyInstaller+souphttpsrc SSL story than MinGW; ships libgiognutls.dll as the GIO TLS backend that souphttpsrc needs.

decisions.decision: Spike scope minimal — one playbin3 + souphttpsrc HTTPS + autoaudiosink; HLS/yt-dlp/cover-art/ICY propagation deferred to Phase 44
decisions.rationale: Validates the three ROADMAP success criteria verbatim with smallest DLL/plugin surface to document. Start-broad-prune-later strategy — iteration 1 collects every installed plugin, then hand-prunes after green.

decisions.decision: Paste-back execution model with ≤5 iteration budget + explicit escalation clause
decisions.rationale: Claude is on Linux; PyInstaller bundling has to happen on Windows. Empirical questions A5 (plugin subdir naming), A7 (girepository version), A10 (audio device) resolve on iteration 1's paste-back. Escalation: if iteration 5 fails on a reason outside the 10 documented failure modes, STOP and revisit scope.

decisions.decision: T-43-01 listen-key leak mitigation: URL query string redacted to '?<redacted>' in smoke_test.py BEFORE any logging; test_url.txt gitignored; artifacts/*.log gitignored; negative-grep gate on findings doc commit
decisions.rationale: AA listen key in test URL is secret. Must never leak into committed artifacts or paste-backs. Mitigation threads through every wave.

decisions.decision: Start-broad plugin collection on iteration 1, prune via exclude_plugins fnmatch on final iteration
decisions.rationale: Hand-curating the plugin list on iteration 1 breaks playbin3's autoplug discovery. Let PyInstaller's stock hook-gi.repository.Gst.py collect via live registry, then subtract after smoke passes. Documented in 43-RESEARCH.md.

next_action: User switches to Windows 11 VM. Follow .planning/phases/43-gstreamer-windows-spike/README.md: revert to clean snapshot → install MSIs → populate test_url.txt → `.\build.ps1` → paste artifacts\smoke.log + audibility verdict into Claude. Next Claude diffs SPIKE_OK / SPIKE_FAIL markers against 43-RESEARCH.md §'Known failure modes' and applies targeted fixes.
context_notes: Auto-chain discuss→plan→execute ran cleanly. Wave 1 landed all four build artifacts from 43-RESEARCH.md code-ready skeletons verbatim (no improvisation — skeletons had exact Tree()/binaries/datas blocks, rthook env-var ordering, exit-code contract). The spike now waits on physical user action; Claude cannot bundle PyInstaller for Windows from Linux. When the user pastes smoke.log, the next Claude walks the 10-row failure-mode→fix table in 43-RESEARCH.md, picks the matching row based on the SPIKE_FAIL reason code, and applies targeted edits to 43-spike.spec or runtime_hook.py. If iteration 5 fails on an unknown reason: STOP, escalate per 43-02-PLAN.md. D-14 reminder: do NOT modify anything under musicstreamer/. No pytest install (spike is freestanding). Auto-chain flag workflow._auto_chain_active has been cleared (set to false) — resuming will not auto-execute.
